findings.title: Misleading error message when session has no org
findings.description: When orgId is null (no session org), the guard returns 'Task does not belong to your organization', which is misleading — the real issue is that there is no authenticated session. In code-review.ts the equivalent `requireOrgMatch` helper distinguishes this by showing `org_id must match session org (none)`. Both files should use a consistent, accurate message for the null-session fail-closed case.

findings.title: Null-org test skips message content assertion
findings.description: The 'returns forbidden when session has no org (fail-closed)' test only asserts `payload.error === 'forbidden'` but does not check `payload.message`, unlike the code-review tests which verify the message contains 'none'. Adding a message assertion would catch the misleading message reported above and keep test thoroughness consistent across the two test files.
